before.keyLabText: Key Laboratory FindingsLaboratory review demonstrates White blood cell count is 7 point 8 thousand per microliter, which is within the expected range. Hemoglobin is 13 point 1 grams per deciliter. High-sensitivity troponin is 4, which remains within the normal range and lowers concern for myocardial injury in the current snapshot. Hemoglobin A one C is elevated at 8 point 2 percent, supporting suboptimal diabetic control. Fasting glucose is 186 milligrams per deciliter, which remains above goal. L D L cholesterol remains above goal at 118 milligrams per deciliter. Creatinine is 0 point 94 milligrams per deciliter. Estimated G F R is 72, suggesting preserved renal function.
